title: Keep replay deduplication partition-local across checkpoints
prompt:
Our stream gateway has a replay guard in front of the side-effecting handlers. It looked healthy in the single-partition soak, but the multi-partition rollout is losing legitimate events: when eight assigned partitions all delivered sequence 700, only one was reported as accepted. We have also reproduced an unseen late event being rejected after restoring a checkpoint when another partition had already used that numeric sequence. Advancing one busy partition must not age data out of a quiet partition.

Please repair the shipped implementation without changing its public API. `observe(partition, sequence)` is linearizable for concurrent callers and returns ACCEPTED for the first observation, DUPLICATE for an already retained observation, or TOO_OLD when the sequence is below that partition's retained window. For a window of W and partition high watermark H, the inclusive retained range is H-W+1 through H: unseen late arrivals in that range are accepted, advancing H evicts older observations for that partition, and at most W observations per partition are retained. Partition sequence spaces are independent even when their numeric values match. The internal `EventPosition` value identity and hash therefore use partition and sequence together. Partition names are non-empty and sequence numbers are non-negative.

Checkpoints must remain deterministic, portable strings and restore the configured window, every partition high watermark, retained observations, and gaps. Restored instances have exactly the same duplicate, late-arrival, and eviction behavior, including for UTF-8 partition names. Keep the implementation JDK-only and free of clocks, sleeps, randomness, and external services. `TestMain.java`, `tests/test_source_contract.py`, and `test.sh` are protected acceptance files; do not modify them. Run `bash test.sh` from this directory; verification must remain offline and deterministic.

full_distill.primary_skill: trace composite event identity through a synchronized bounded deduplication index and its checkpoint codec while preserving partition-local sequence windows
full_distill.baseline_evidence: java TestMain.java exits 1 because observations at the same numeric sequence in different partitions are reported as duplicates, including concurrent first deliveries and unseen late arrivals after checkpoint restore
full_distill.success_contract: concurrent observation, partition-local eviction, late arrivals, and deterministic checkpoint restore all preserve an independent bounded sequence window per partition with no false cross-partition duplicate decisions
adversarial_factors: sequential and barrier-released concurrent deliveries reuse the same numeric sequence across different partitions, so a process-global sequence-only index loses valid events
one partition advances far beyond another while the quiet partition's retained duplicate and unseen late sequence remain live, rejecting global-watermark eviction
checkpoint round trips preserve high-watermark gaps and UTF-8 partition names, then introduce late arrivals whose sequence exists in another partition, rejecting state clearing and incomplete restore
same-partition duplicate storms still admit exactly one event and retained counts stay within the configured window, rejecting a fix that disables deduplication
